tps.title: this.attributes.last_triggered unreliable in automation conditions
tps.reason: Fixed to use state_attr() for consistent behavior

tps.title: Silent defaults mask sensor failures with plausible values
tps.reason: Added availability templates to HR/O2 baselines and room temp alert

tps.title: Room temperature float(0) silently disables alerts
tps.reason: Added availability template so sensor goes unavailable instead of false

tps.title: PIR effectively disabled - radar_available never goes false
tps.reason: Fixed: changed from state check to label_entities() configuration check

tps.title: Comment says 2min cooldown, code uses 5sec threshold
tps.reason: Fixed comment to say debounce/5 seconds, matching actual code

tps.title: Privacy mode reads nonexistent open_sensors attribute
tps.reason: Added active_sensors attribute and updated reference

tps.title: Short cycling detection broken - runtime = 0 when idle
tps.reason: Fixed: now uses trigger.from_state.last_changed for runtime calculation

tps.title: Delta T 30-day average is hardcoded placeholder 18.5
tps.reason: Made placeholder explicit with clear YAML comment

tps.title: Availability vs state health check inconsistency
tps.reason: Fixed: added flue_temp_available check to availability template to match state template

tps.title: HVAC lockout restore overwrites manual thermostat changes
tps.reason: Added condition to skip restore if user manually adjusted thermostat during lockout

tps.title: as_datetime() not guarded against malformed timestamps
tps.reason: Added None check for as_datetime() return value before passing to as_local()

tps.title: max_tokens increase from 500 to 2000
tps.reason: Real fix — Gemini 2.5 Flash uses ~500 thinking tokens from budget, leaving only 2 visible tokens at max_tokens=500. Verified via LiteLLM spend logs showing completion_tokens=496 with truncated output.

tps.title: Outing start automation triggers when either tracker leaves home without stabilizing guard
tps.reason: Valid concern confirmed by real-world data — GPS bounces and StatZon states from iCloud can cause false triggers. Adding a for: duration on the trigger is a good suggestion.

tps.title: Media suppression treats unavailable as active via not is_state off
tps.reason: Fixed all instances to check explicit playback states instead of not-off

tps.title: Calendar response indexing fragile - dict key lookup raises before default runs
tps.reason: Fixed to use .get() with fallback empty dict

tps.title: LLM call missing continue_on_error
tps.reason: Added continue_on_error: true to prevent 60s timeout from blocking entire report

tps.title: motion_only can go negative
tps.reason: Fixed with [v, 0] | max clamp in both locations

tps.title: Buzzer not guaranteed off if tier3 interrupted
tps.reason: Added defensive switch.turn_off in episode_end cleanup

tps.title: stale_list Jinja loop variable scoping bug
tps.reason: Fixed with namespace() pattern

tps.title: notify.persistent_notification vs persistent_notification.create inconsistency
tps.reason: Standardized to persistent_notification.create

tps.title: Leak alert throttle uses .last_changed instead of stored datetime value
tps.reason: Fixed to use states() value with unknown/unavailable guard

tps.title: current_threshold attribute does not exist on binary_sensor
tps.reason: Fixed to reference min_threshold which exists

tps.title: Monthly summary reads post-reset meter on day 1
tps.reason: Added snapshot automation at 23:59:50 on last day of month and input_number helper

tps.title: Leak volume can go negative after meter reset
tps.reason: Fixed with [v, 0] | max clamp

tps.title: Both calendar update automations try to build a list inside a Jinja for loop using dates = dates + [event_date]
tps.reason: Fixed with namespace(dates=[]) pattern in both garbage and recycling automations

tps.title: The task-count and task-list templates use the same loop-local reassignment pattern
tps.reason: Fixed with namespace(count=0) and namespace(tasks=[]) patterns

tps.title: Both date-update automations index directly into the response object
tps.reason: Fixed with .get() guard pattern: calendar_events.get('calendar.minneapolis_mn_usa', {}).get('events', [])

tps.title: The weekly_activity_counter_reset automation startup condition restricts to 00:01 window
tps.reason: Fixed with idempotency check using existing reset date helpers instead of time window

tps.title: The automation references sensor.fresh_air_available but the sensor is named Fresh Air Available Optimized
tps.reason: Fixed both references to sensor.fresh_air_available_optimized

tps.title: Fresh Air Available Optimized converts indoor PM2.5 with float(0) treating unavailable as clean air
tps.reason: Fixed to filter out unavailable values using reject('in', ['unknown', 'unavailable']) before taking max

tps.title: Outdoor PM2.5 EPA Corrected availability only checks timestamps not state validity
tps.reason: Fixed availability to check both state validity and timestamp freshness

tps.title: Unused variable current_state in alert_level attribute
tps.reason: Removed dead variable

tps.title: All three automations dereference the calendar response with direct indexing without guard
tps.reason: Fixed all 3 automations with guarded events variable and updated 6 references

tps.title: Per-room attributes coerce unavailable to 0.0 with float(0)
tps.reason: Fixed all 8 per-room attributes across both sensors to return none for unavailable values

tps.title: Template list accumulation inside loop will not persist
tps.reason: Real bug: Jinja loop scoping. Need namespace() to accumulate across iterations.

tps.title: Leaderboard renders negative winning scores with a malformed plus sign
tps.reason: Real formatting bug, easy fix.

tps.title: Malformed or missing AI output is treated as a real litter visit
tps.reason: Real bug: should fallback to no_detection or skip counting on malformed LLM response.

tps.title: Fresh-air suggestion unreachable when all windows closed
tps.reason: Real logic bug in condition ordering.

tps.title: Stale device list attribute is serialized as a string, breaking report iteration
tps.reason: Real bug: string serialization breaks downstream template iteration.

tps.title: Trigger-based template sensors do not initialize on Home Assistant startup
tps.reason: Real bug: need homeassistant start trigger for PID sensors.

tps.title: Unmapped behaviors are never logged if they score as zero
tps.reason: Real bug: defeats the purpose of tracking unmapped behaviors.

tps.title: Misleading notification can report Both Dogs/Cats when only one pet had a negative event
tps.reason: Real logic error in notification template.

tps.title: CO2 control comparisons use float(0) fallbacks, which can turn fans off on sensor unavailability
tps.reason: Real safety issue: unavailable sensors treated as 0 CO2, suppressing ventilation.

tps.title: Sensor failures are treated as safe values and can incorrectly suppress ventilation
tps.reason: Same root cause as above -- real safety concern.

tps.title: Basement CO2 control also treats unavailable readings as zero
tps.reason: Same pattern, different sensor. Real bug.

tps.title: Template condition can fail when `event_type` is missing
tps.reason: Valid concern. During HA startup/restore, event entities may have incomplete attributes. Using attributes.get('event_type') would be more defensive.

tps.title: Input boolean forces enabled state on every restart
tps.reason: Valid. If someone disables buttons intentionally, a restart would silently re-enable them. Removing initial: true lets HA restore the previous state.

tps.title: Conditional card can render an empty secondary field when done timestamp is unavailable
tps.reason: Valid — the washer done alert card shows blank secondary if input_datetime.washer_cycle_done_at hasn't been populated yet. Fixing with an else fallback.

tps.title: Conditional card can render an empty secondary field when dryer done timestamp is unavailable
tps.reason: Same issue on dryer card. Fixing both with else fallback text.

tps.title: Missing alert cards for heart rate conditions
tps.reason: Real issue — when high/low HR alerts fired, All Clear disappeared but no replacement card showed. Fixed by adding conditional mushroom-template-cards for both states.

tps.title: Heart rate gauge min=60 hides sub-60 readings
tps.reason: Real issue — low HR alert could fire below 60 BPM but gauge couldn't display it. Fixed by changing min to 40.

tps.title: No complementary cards for sock/base station disconnect states
tps.reason: Real issue — device disconnect left blank section. Fixed by adding grey mushroom-template-cards with actionable guidance text.

tps.title: Garden color logic prioritizes dry-run states over lockout, potentially masking lockout state
tps.reason: Real issue — lockout is higher-priority than power status. Fixed by reordering conditions in all 3 fountain tiles (Garden, Back Porch, Stairwell) to check lockout first.

tps.title: float(50) default masks unavailable sensors as healthy in health gate state template
tps.reason: Real bug. Unavailable position sensors defaulted to 50 (above all warning thresholds), silently hiding offline plants as healthy. Fixed by checking for unknown/unavailable/none before evaluating.

tps.title: float(50) default masks unavailable sensors in all_needing_water attribute template
tps.reason: Same bug as state template — unavailable sensors default to 50 and get silently skipped. Fixed with unavailable guard and continue.

tps.title: Threshold boundary inconsistency: Today's Actions uses <= while per-plant cards use strict <
tps.reason: Real logic bug. Today's Actions used pos <= limits.crit / pos <= limits.warn while health gate and all per-plant cards used strict <. A plant at exactly the threshold value would show differently in different parts of the dashboard. Fixed to use < consistently.

tps.title: Hardcoded secret in `password`
tps.reason: Real hardcoded password for OpenObserve ingest. Fixed by moving to !secret reference.

tps.title: Sensitive credential appears embedded in YAML configuration
tps.reason: Duplicate of hardcoded secret finding. Fixed by moving to !secret reference.

tps.title: Humidity automations treat unavailable sensor readings as valid low values
tps.reason: Real concern. float(50) when sensor unavailable means emergency exhaust won't fire (50 < 85 ceiling). Should be addressed in a follow-up — either gate on sensor availability or use a default above the ceiling to fail-safe.

tps.title: Watering detection can repeatedly fire on every state update after a watering event
tps.reason: Real issue. After watering, current - 2h_min > threshold stays true for up to 2 hours, causing repeated writes to input_datetime and duplicate log entries on each sensor update. Needs debounce.

tps.title: Task UID lookup never persists match inside Jinja loop
tps.reason: {% set found_uid %} inside {% for %} is loop-scoped in Jinja2 3.x. No namespace() used, so found_uid always empty — upsert always creates duplicates.

tps.title: Completion-by-name uses the same broken loop-scoped UID search
tps.reason: Same Jinja loop-scoping bug as upsert. task_to_complete_uid always empty, complete_by_name never matches.

tps.title: Shared task cache is not keyed by todo list entity
tps.reason: Single input_text cache always populated from default list. Callers passing different list_entity get stale/wrong results.

tps.title: Task cache storage is too small for JSON item lists
tps.reason: input_text max: 255. Single todo item is 100+ chars JSON. Cache fits 1-2 items, effectively dead code for real lists.

tps.title: Priority input is accepted but never used
tps.reason: Priority field accepted by scripts but never passed to todo.add_item or todo.update_item calls. Dead configuration.

tps.title: Consolidated plant state is capped at 255 characters and will overflow
tps.reason: 13 plants × ~60-70 chars each = 713-947 chars. input_text max 255 silently truncates, producing invalid JSON.

tps.title: Parallel read-modify-write updates can lose plant state changes
tps.reason: mode: parallel with read-modify-write on shared input_text. Classic TOCTOU race — concurrent plant updates overwrite each other.

tps.title: Inactive-state script repeats the same unsafe parallel state update pattern
tps.reason: Same mode: parallel + read-modify-write race on same input_text entity. Can also race against set_active script.

tps.title: Shared state mutation script is explicitly configured for parallel execution
tps.reason: mode: parallel is the root cause enabling the race. Comments claim independence but all scripts share the same input_text entity.

tps.title: Task creation runs even when no task text was recognized
tps.reason: Service call fires unconditionally before {% if task %} guard in speech template. Creates tasks with empty names.

tps.title: Unsafe indexing into todo.get_items response can break the voice response flow
tps.reason: Dict key lookup fails before default() can catch it if entity ID is invalid. Jinja default only catches undefined, not KeyError.

tps.title: Task completion service is invoked even when the spoken task slot is missing
tps.reason: Same unconditional-action pattern as task creation. Service fires with empty input before speech checks the slot.

tps.title: State injector does not actually modify entity state, so tests won't exercise automation triggers
tps.reason: Script only calls system_log.write and input_text.set_value. Comment says "for now, log what we would inject." Never sets target entity state.

tps.title: Mock API assertion payload can be truncated by 255-character input_text limit
tps.reason: 9-key JSON dict via to_json easily exceeds input_text max: 255. Silent truncation produces broken JSON.

tps.title: Test results log is prone to truncation and loss of assertions
tps.reason: Same 255-char limit with cumulative append pattern. After 2-3 state injections, accumulated string truncates.

tps.title: Parallel result appends can race and overwrite each other
tps.reason: mode: parallel, max: 10 with read-modify-write on input_text. Textbook TOCTOU race.

tps.title: Resume success check runs immediately after vacuum.start and can misclassify successes as failures
tps.reason: No delay or wait_template between vacuum.start and state check. Service returns on dispatch, not when vacuum transitions. Will almost always see old state.

tps.title: Recovery automation ignores the global Donetick enable switch
tps.reason: No condition block checking input_boolean.donetick_system_enabled, unlike other automations in the same package.

tps.title: Placeholder NFC tag ID leaves the automation non-functional
tps.reason: Literal PLACEHOLDER_MAILBOX_NFC_TAG_ID will never match an actual NFC scan event. Automation is dead code.

tps.title: Automation writes to an entity that is not declared in this package
tps.reason: Writes to input_datetime.mailbox_last_delivery declared in smart_mailbox_detection.yaml. Valid coupling concern.

tps.title: Streak logic does not implement the documented 4-hour grace period
tps.reason: Comment documents 4-hour grace period but code does simple date comparison. Doc and implementation disagree.

tps.title: Eligibility binary sensor uses an inappropriate device class
tps.reason: device_class: connectivity for a scan-cooldown sensor is semantically wrong. Affects icon/display in HA frontend.

tps.title: Dry-run duration note is computed from the recovery timestamp, not the incident start
tps.reason: relative_time(last_changed) on sensor that just flipped to off gives ~2 min (the for: delay), not actual incident duration.

tps.title: Back porch completion note has the same incorrect duration calculation
tps.reason: Same relative_time(last_changed) pattern on kitchen_fountain_dry_run_detected. Same issue as garden fountain.

tps.title: Stairwell completion note has the same incorrect duration calculation
tps.reason: Same relative_time(last_changed) pattern on stairwell_fountain_dry_run_detected.

tps.title: Auto-reset checks yesterday instead of the actual collection day
tps.reason: Fires at noon on collection day but checks yesterday's date in calendar. Should check today since trucks come today. Resets one day late.

tps.title: Undo action does not clear the completion guard, preventing re-completion after undo
tps.reason: Undo turns off bin status but doesn't reset activity_reset datetime. Guard blocks re-completion via NFC after undo.

tps.title: Weekly reset idempotency guard only checks the garbage reset marker
tps.reason: Only checks garbage marker. If garbage reset succeeds but recycling fails, re-run is blocked. Should check both markers.

tps.title: Next collection date helper is never cleared when no calendar event is found
tps.reason: Choose block only writes when next_date is not none. No else/default branch to clear stale value.

tps.title: Rule misses lowercase and mixed-case header names despite HTTP being case-insensitive
tps.reason: Correct — HTTP header names are case-insensitive. Rewrote rule to use regex constraint (?i)['\"]access-control-allow-origin['\"] so any case variant matches. Added 3 case-variant tests.

tps.title: Detection is limited to setHeader and header missing common response APIs
tps.reason: Valid — expanded METHOD constraint to include 'set' (covers Next.js/Fetch Response.headers.set style). Added fixture test.

tps.title: Rule misses common object-based and header-map forms that also set Access-Control-Allow-Origin
tps.reason: Real coverage gap for object-literal forms like res.set({'Access-Control-Allow-Origin': '*'}). Deferring to a follow-up issue — ast-grep object-key matching requires more complex rule syntax.

fps.title: Entity IDs do not match aliases defined earlier
fps.reason: All entity IDs verified to match slugified names in actual HA registry

fps.title: Folded multiline Jinja blocks may emit extra whitespace
fps.reason: HA normalizes template output whitespace consistently

fps.title: open_sensors parsing assumes mapping, Alarmo may send list
fps.reason: Alarmo always sends dict mapping, code already handles three formats defensively

fps.title: now() causes unnecessary 1-min refresh cycles
fps.reason: By design - runtime counter needs live-updating display, 1-min refresh is appropriate

fps.title: Entity ID cross-references may not match slugified names
fps.reason: All entity IDs verified against actual HA registry - all match

fps.title: Alert automations lack deduplication on startup/reload
fps.reason: mode:single + state change triggers already handle this - no duplication on restart

fps.title: is match() not supported in HA Jinja
fps.reason: HA Jinja DOES support is match() as a test - this is a known HA extension

fps.title: truncate(3500) conflicts with 2800 char instruction
fps.reason: Different purposes: 2800 is LLM soft guideline, 3500 is Slack hard safety cap

fps.title: No target allowlist in Slack link construction
fps.reason: By design - callers are trusted internal scripts, not user input

fps.title: No url validation in Slack link construction
fps.reason: All URLs are fixed internal HA paths, not user-controlled

fps.title: notification script calls pass scalar target inside data instead of target mapping
fps.reason: Calibration correctly disputed this — target is a parameter OF the script.notify_mobile script, not a HA service call target mapping. This is idiomatic HA usage.

fps.title: REST fetch + JSON parse + 200 events + prompt build + second REST call concentrates processing
fps.reason: Runs once daily at 8pm. 200 events is trivially small for Jinja iteration. The 'two REST calls' are fetch data + send to LLM, which is the minimum for this workflow. No performance issue exists.

fps.title: Camera event data forwarded to multiple external sinks without masking or minimization
fps.reason: This is a personal home automation system. All 'external' sinks are self-hosted and private: Slack is a private workspace, housemem is a local service, mobile push goes to the homeowner's phone, calendar is local HA. There are no third-party services receiving this data. Privacy controls are inappropriate here.

fps.title: Cross-file pattern of collecting rich behavior/event data and forwarding to external destinations without clear minimization boundaries
fps.reason: All destinations are self-hosted private services (local Slack workspace, local housemem, homeowner's phone, local HA calendar). No third-party data exposure. Privacy controls would add complexity with zero benefit for a personal home system.

fps.title: from_json({}) may not be valid Jinja2 syntax
fps.reason: Context7 HA docs confirm from_json accepts a default parameter directly. This is the documented pattern for HA 2025.x+.

fps.title: Calendar entity renamed from llm_vision_timeline to llmvision_activity_log
fps.reason: Intentional rename — old entity was wrong name. Already deployed and verified working.

fps.title: Cross-file from_json pattern consistency concern
fps.reason: All packages were updated in the same commit (4e63dc5) to use from_json(default) pattern consistently.

fps.title: Calendar entity rename may break existing data
fps.reason: Intentional change, old entity name was incorrect. New calendar entity created and verified.

fps.title: The automation sends four Slack notifications sequentially with one-second delays between them. This design adds at least several seconds of avoidable latency.
fps.reason: The 4-message split was explicitly designed to solve a 5500-char Slack truncation problem. Combining back into fewer messages recreates the original bug. The 1-second delays prevent Slack rate limiting and ensure message ordering. This is intentional, not a performance issue.

fps.title: logbook.log service calls target entities by setting entity_id inside data instead of using a target block
fps.reason: logbook.log is a special service that does not support the target: block pattern. The entity_id in data is used to associate the log entry with an entity for display purposes, not to target the service call. This is the correct and documented usage for logbook.log.

fps.title: silently swallows JSON parse failures from AI output
fps.reason: The calibration review correctly disputed this. The code has explicit fallback handling with safe defaults — this is intentional defensive programming, not silent failure.

fps.title: mis-normalizes single mapping objects as iterables
fps.reason: Calibration correctly identified this as based on a hallucinated line of code. The actual implementation handles this correctly.

fps.title: Package detection debounce 15s may miss slow-moving intruders
fps.reason: The debounce is on the notification cooldown (last_triggered check), not on detection. Alarmo itself handles detection instantly. The cooldown just prevents notification spam for the same package sitting there.

fps.title: JSON trim in notification may produce invalid JSON
fps.reason: Line 795 uses truncate(255) on sensor_name which is a plain text string (friendly names joined with commas), not JSON. No JSON parsing downstream.

fps.title: Notification device validation - notify service could fail silently
fps.reason: script.notify_mobile is a wrapper script that handles device routing, target validation, and channel selection internally. It's not a raw notify service call.

fps.title: notify_mobile failure blocks entire pipeline
fps.reason: P1 critical alerts intentionally do NOT have continue_on_error on notify_mobile — if mobile push fails for a security alert, the pipeline SHOULD stop so it's visible. Slack already has continue_on_error:true as secondary channel. Other priority levels route through script wrappers that handle failures internally.

fps.title: Person dedup 60s window may miss re-entries
fps.reason: The dedup window is actually 1800 seconds (30 minutes), not 60 seconds. Line 215: (now() - as_datetime(last_notified_time)).total_seconds() < 1800. 30 min is appropriate for suppressing repeated detections of the same person at the same camera.

fps.title: Entity IDs may not exist (phantom references)
fps.reason: All entities reference _2 suffix which is documented at lines 5-11 as replacement device. Comments explicitly state: 'Base Entities from Owlet Integration (replacement device, _2 suffix)'. These are real entities from the Owlet integration.

fps.title: Naming mismatches between sensors
fps.reason: The 'mismatches' are intentional aliasing — the entire purpose of Section 1 (lines 29-108) is to create standardized names (e.g., sensor.ottilie_heart_rate) that reference the integration's auto-generated names (e.g., sensor.ottilie_s_owlet_heart_rate_2). This is by design.

fps.title: Laundry peak deviation race condition between peak_power and deviation_pct
fps.reason: The trigger-based template sensor (lines 825-848) reads input_select.washer_laundry_state and axis sensor states atomically in a single Jinja template evaluation. There is no race — HA evaluates the entire template as one unit when the trigger fires. The peak tracking uses this.state (previous value) which is also atomic.

fps.title: Fridge entity IDs may not exist
fps.reason: sensor.kitchen_refrigerator_power and sensor.basement_refrigerator_power are ESPHome Sonoff S31 devices explicitly listed in the file header (lines 16-17). They are real hardware with stable entity IDs.

fps.title: No global monitoring disable check in templates
fps.reason: All automations already check input_boolean.appliance_monitoring_enabled (e.g., line 1183). Template sensors intentionally do NOT check this toggle because they need to remain available for Energy Dashboard integration and history stats even when notifications are suppressed.

fps.title: Entity ID mismatches in sensor references
fps.reason: All referenced sensors (living_room_temperature, living_room_stove_left_temperature, etc.) are real entities from Zigbee temperature sensors and the ecobee. The flue sensor entity (sensor.wood_stove_flue_monitor_wood_stove_flue_temperature) has a verbose name because ESPHome auto-generates it from device name + sensor name.

fps.title: HVAC lockout setpoint overwrite clobbers user changes
fps.reason: Lines 1067-1075 already guard against this. The restore automation checks: abs(current_setpoint - expected_lockout_setpoint) < 1. If the user manually changed the setpoint during lockout, the condition fails and the automation does NOT restore, respecting the user's manual override.

fps.title: Circulation fan should not run during HVAC heat
fps.reason: climate.set_fan_mode to 'on' only controls the blower fan, not the heating mode. The ecobee continues to manage heating independently. Running the fan during stove use distributes heat — it doesn't conflict with HVAC heating. If furnace kicks in, fan was already on, which is fine.

fps.title: Ecobee preset interaction concerns
fps.reason: Speculative concern. The ecobee presets (comfort profiles) operate on schedules. The lockout automation saves/restores the numerical setpoint, which is orthogonal to preset names. If a preset changes the setpoint, the lockout captures the new value. No conflict.

fps.title: Sensitive data to external services
fps.reason: Both LiteLLM and housemem are self-hosted on local LAN with TLS/bearer auth

fps.title: Stale report mixes offline vs stale counts
fps.reason: Report clearly labels both sections separately

fps.title: calendar.get_events uses target entity_id which matches HA style guidance
fps.reason: Not a finding, just a positive observation

fps.title: Relay is configured to always turn on after reboot
fps.reason: Intentional: refrigerator relay MUST default on after power loss to keep food safe

fps.title: Relay restore mode 'ALWAYS_ON' can cause unexpected activation
fps.reason: Same as above -- intentional for refrigerator appliance monitoring

fps.title: Fallback AP is configured without captive_portal component
fps.reason: Fallback AP activates only when WiFi fails -- that's its intended purpose. Not a security issue.

fps.title: Boot-strap pin GPIO0 is used as a button input
fps.reason: Standard Sonoff/Tuya ESP8266 design. Every off-the-shelf relay board uses GPIO0 for button.

fps.title: Hard-coded sunrise and sunset times break true circadian behavior
fps.reason: Intentional for ADHD-friendly consistent scheduling. Not a bug.

fps.title: Hard-coded sunrise and sunset times will drift from actual daylight conditions
fps.reason: Same as above -- intentional design choice for predictable lighting schedule.

fps.title: homeassistant.debug: false key
fps.reason: Silently ignored by HA, no validation failure. Not a real bug.

fps.title: Fallback AP enabled permanently
fps.reason: That's literally how ESPHome fallback AP works - only activates when WiFi fails

fps.title: GPIO15 on front-porch-air-quality
fps.reason: Not present in this file - false positive, wrong file referenced

fps.title: Shower-ended humidity recheck is incorrect
fps.reason: Logic is correct: shower_ended checks threshold at entry

fps.title: Basement shower not triggering fan immediately
fps.reason: Shower activity IS highest-priority choose branch. Triggers correctly.

fps.title: HVAC re-enable watching hvac_mode attribute
fps.reason: Valid trigger: climate entities expose hvac_mode as attribute. Works correctly.

fps.title: wifi.domain .5745.house
fps.reason: Correct DNS domain for network. Not mDNS .local issue.

fps.title: Daily energy depends on HA time
fps.reason: Standard ESPHome pattern using homeassistant.time. Correct approach.

fps.title: Cross-package helper dependencies
fps.reason: Defined in core config, working as designed. Not undeclared.

fps.title: Unused homeassistant_time
fps.reason: Confirmed used by total_daily_energy sensor. Not unused.

fps.title: Leak detection may be permanently disabled by checking a non-existent statistics attribute
fps.reason: source_value_valid is a real HA statistics sensor attribute. Confirmed it exists on sensor.water_flow_rate_1hr_avg (currently false with age_coverage_ratio 0.64). The guard is working as intended to prevent false alarms on stale data.

fps.title: Fold-entity-row is used to wrap non-entity cards, which is likely unsupported
fps.reason: custom:fold-entity-row is the de facto HA community standard for collapsible sections in a grid/sections layout. While technically designed for use inside an entities card, it renders reliably standalone in Lovelace sections views. All HA dashboard guides and HACS documentation show this usage pattern. Not a real runtime issue.

fps.title: Same non-portable `%-d` date format repeated in diagnostics view
fps.reason: Home Assistant runs exclusively on Linux (the HA OS is Alpine/Debian-based). The %-d strftime flag is a GNU/Linux extension and always works in this context. Cross-platform portability to Windows is not a concern for HA dashboard templates.

fps.title: Settings section also misuses fold-entity-row outside an entities card context
fps.reason: Same as the first finding — standalone fold-entity-row in a grid section is the accepted HA community pattern and renders correctly.

fps.title: Additional fold-entity-row usages in diagnostics likely break card rendering
fps.reason: Same as above — accepted HA community pattern, not a rendering issue in practice.

fps.title: Jinja templates in tile color field
fps.reason: HA tile cards support Jinja templates in the color field since 2023.x. This works correctly in production.

fps.title: Platform-specific strftime directive may break on non-Unix environments
fps.reason: HA runs on Linux/Docker. %-d is supported. Not a real portability concern for this deployment.

fps.title: Template used in tile card color may not be supported
fps.reason: HA tile cards support Jinja templates in the color field since 2023.x. Works correctly in production.

fps.title: Power Monitoring section title and layout suggest multiple gauges, but only Garden is implemented
fps.reason: Quorum reviewed a truncated excerpt. The full file has gauges for all 3 fountains (Garden, Porch, Stairwell).

fps.title: Management section exposes reset action for only one fountain despite dashboard covering three
fps.reason: Same truncation issue — the full file has reset buttons for all 3 fountains (Reset Garden, Reset Porch, Reset Stair).

fps.title: Unsupported Jinja templating used in tile color field
fps.reason: HA tile cards support Jinja templates in the color field since 2023.x. Works correctly in production.

fps.title: Repeated unsupported Jinja templating in second tile color field
fps.reason: Same as above — HA tile cards support Jinja in color. Duplicate of first finding.

fps.title: Markdown card content uses Jinja date template that may not render
fps.reason: HA markdown cards fully support Jinja templates including now().strftime(). This is core HA functionality.

fps.title: Platform-specific strftime directive may break on non-Unix environments
fps.reason: HA runs on Linux/Docker. %-d is supported. Not a portability concern.

fps.title: Platform-specific strftime directives may break date rendering
fps.reason: HA runs on Linux/Docker. %-d and %-I are supported. Not a portability concern for this deployment.

fps.title: Use of state_not in conditional card is not valid Lovelace syntax
fps.reason: state_not IS valid in HA conditional cards, supported since 2022.x. Works correctly in production.

fps.title: Broken navigation path to Diagnostics view
fps.reason: The path /entertainment-fan-control/diagnostics matches the dashboard slug exactly. Navigation works correctly.

fps.title: Broken back navigation path to Fan Control view
fps.reason: Same as above — /entertainment-fan-control/fan-control matches the dashboard slug. Navigation works correctly.

fps.title: backyard_tree_enabled toggle gates both front and back yard trees
fps.reason: Intentional. Both outdoor trees are on the same watering circuit and monitored as a pair. The toggle controls whether outdoor tree monitoring is active at all, not individual trees.

fps.title: Only 3 plant cards visible, missing cards for remaining plants
fps.reason: Quorum only saw a truncated excerpt of the dashboard file. All 12+ plant cards exist with correct per-category thresholds — the review just didn't see the full file.

fps.title: Invalid `if` condition syntax in automations likely prevents startup delay logic from working
fps.reason: HA 2023.x+ supports trigger matching in if actions — `platform: homeassistant` / `event: start` is valid syntax to check which trigger fired. This pattern is used across many automations in this codebase.

fps.title: Same invalid `if` trigger-style condition appears in PID exhaust automation
fps.reason: Same as above — valid HA trigger matching syntax in if actions.

fps.title: Deprecated singular `trigger:` -- use `triggers:` instead
fps.reason: trigger: is the standard, supported HA automation key. It has not been deprecated in favor of triggers:. 100% FP rate in quorum precedent history.

fps.title: Top-level sensor platforms are missing the required parent key
fps.reason: Review saw code excerpt, not actual file. Real file has proper sensor: parent key — filter and statistics entries are correctly nested under the existing sensor: block.

fps.title: Comment claims all 4 PLT-1B watering automations exist, but only one is defined
fps.reason: Review only saw one example automation in the excerpt. All 4 PLT-1B automations (beetle_peperomia, watermelon_peperomia, lime_tree, spider_plant_hanging) exist in the actual file.

fps.title: Cache freshness check reads last_changed as an attribute instead of entity metadata
fps.reason: Wrong line range cited (L189-199 is fountain handler, not cache logic). state_attr returning None is handled gracefully by cache_age=999 fallback — cache just always misses, no breakage.

fps.title: User attribution relies on brittle device_id substring matching
fps.reason: device_id in mobile_app_notification_action events is the standard and only practical approach for HA companion app attribution. Stable IDs, graceful fallback to "Unknown User".

fps.title: Test runner leaves previous system flags enabled across runs
fps.reason: Cleanup happens in donetick_test_automation_runner automation (L583-588) which turns off both system-specific flag and global test mode after completion.

fps.title: Task due time is hard-coded instead of using the calendar event start
fps.reason: 7 AM is a reasonable fixed pickup time. Municipal calendar events are all-day with no meaningful start time to use. Intentional design choice.

fps.title: Pattern is too broad and can flag unrelated .set() calls outside HTTP response context
fps.reason: The HEADER constraint regex (?i)['\"]access-control-allow-origin['\"] limits this very strongly — theoretical FP requires someone to call myMap.set('Access-Control-Allow-Origin', '*') on a non-response object, which does not appear in real code. Accepted breadth cost for catching Fetch Response.headers.set.
